takeaways: REITs dominate senior housing M&A — Welltower sourced 37 of 41 Q1 deals off-market and Ventas closed $1.7B with 90%+ relationship-driven. Off-market scouting is now table stakes for operators competing on quality assets.

Fed cut rates 25 bps to 3.5–3.75% but the 10-Year Treasury is stuck at 4.00–4.25% — CRE borrowing costs won't fall in lockstep, and 2026 transaction volume is projected up just 16% to $562B as recovery stays measured.
Agentic AI is leaving pilot mode this month — ServiceNow, Adobe, and Xactly all shipped production agents in May, while proptech doubles down on AI-fraud defense after $275M in reported 2025 losses.

items.headline: REITs Dominate Off-Market Senior Living M&A as Relationships Win Deals
items.summary: Welltower sourced 37 of its 41 Q1 transactions off-market and roughly two-thirds of Ventas' $1.7B in 2026 senior living investments were off-market, with 90%+ relationship-driven and 40% from repeat sellers. For mid-sized operators like Lloyd Jones, the implication is clear: marketed processes increasingly leave the best assets on the table — sponsor relationships and data infrastructure are the new cost of entry.

items.headline: Pegasus CEO: No 'Easy Solution' to Senior Living's Supply Gap
items.summary: Senior living inventory growth hit a record low of 0.4% in early 2026 and construction starts have plummeted to levels not seen since 2012, even as demand keeps climbing. Pegasus Senior Living's CEO warns that financing, labor, and construction costs make new supply implausible at scale — a tailwind for existing operators' occupancy and rate growth through 2028.

items.headline: Fed Cuts Rates 25 bps to 3.5–3.75% as Labor Market Cools
items.summary: The April 29 FOMC delivered a widely-anticipated quarter-point cut, bringing the federal funds target to 3.5–3.75%, but most analysts see the Fed holding from here. For CRE, the cut improves clarity on cap rates and refinancing — but the disconnect with long-end rates means commercial mortgage pricing won't fall in lockstep.

items.headline: CRE Capital Markets Enter a Transitional Phase as Liquidity Returns
items.summary: Banks are slowly re-entering the lending market, transaction activity is picking up from recent lows, and valuations appear closer to stabilizing, per a May 13 NAIOP Charlotte read. Confidence is building and capital is flowing again — but rate cuts may ease pressure without rescuing underperforming assets, putting a premium on operator quality.

items.headline: Proptech Rides to the Fight Against AI-Driven Rental and Mortgage Fraud
items.summary: More than 12,000 real estate fraud complaints were filed with the FBI in 2025, totaling over $275M in reported losses — much of it driven by generative AI tools producing fake IDs, paystubs, and bank statements. Proptech vendors are now embedding ML-based document and identity verification into leasing and underwriting workflows, a category every multifamily and senior housing operator should evaluate this year.

items.headline: Agentic AI Deployment Enters Production Reality
items.summary: After two years of pilots, agentic AI is moving into live enterprise workflows in 2026 — automating procurement, pricing, inventory, and back-office processes at scale. VCs invested $16.7B in proptech in 2025 (+68% YoY), with AI-native companies growing at 42% annualized — nearly double non-AI peers.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Report Increased Stress as US Layoffs Hit 10-Year High
items.summary: Many organizations entered 2026 in a very lean state after the largest US layoff wave in a decade, and 71% of leaders now report elevated stress — with 40% considering leaving their jobs. The leadership lesson: as AI flattens hierarchies, retention of senior operators is becoming the binding constraint, not headcount.

items.headline: Active Adult Occupancy Holds at 91.2% Despite Housing Market Volatility
items.summary: Q1 2026 active adult occupancy across 125 markets dipped just 70 basis points to 91.2%, blunting fears that broader housing market softness would hit move-ins. Core senior living customers continue to have the wealth and home equity to fund moves — a signal that demand fundamentals remain decoupled from broader for-sale housing weakness.

items.headline: 44% of Assisted Living Residents Now Live With Alzheimer's or Dementia
items.summary: Nearly half of older adults in AL communities have Alzheimer's or another form of dementia, raising the bar for clinical staffing and memory care design. The affordability gap remains stark: median AL cost was $70,800 in 2024 vs. average 75+ household income of $47,790 — a setup for continued middle-market product innovation.

items.headline: University-Senior Living Partnerships Reach 85–100 Communities
items.summary: Between 85 and 100 senior living communities now sit on or near university campuses with some form of formal relationship — a quickly-growing niche pairing aging-in-community with lifelong learning. For developers, university-affiliated sites are increasingly viewed as a differentiated entry point in capacity-constrained markets.

items.headline: 10-Year Treasury Stabilizes at 4.00–4.25% — CRE Pricing Disconnect Persists
items.summary: Most fixed-rate CRE loans price off the 10-year, which has held in a tight 4.00–4.25% band since mid-2025 even as the Fed cuts short rates. Commercial loan rates currently run 4.97–12.75% depending on asset and credit, with conventional CRE mortgages in the 5.23–8.75% range — meaning sponsors should not underwrite to aggressive rate-down scenarios.

items.headline: 2026 CRE Investment Activity Projected +16% to $562B
items.summary: CBRE expects US commercial real estate investment volume to rise 16% in 2026 to $562B as capital re-engages and bid-ask spreads narrow. The recovery is measured rather than euphoric — well-capitalized operators with off-market sourcing have the biggest edge.

items.headline: ServiceNow and Accenture Launch Forward Deployed Engineering for Agentic AI
items.summary: Announced May 6, the joint program embeds ServiceNow's AI-native FDE team alongside Accenture industry FDEs inside customer environments to take agentic workflows from pilot to production. The model — modeled on Palantir-style FDE work — signals how enterprise agent deployment will actually scale across complex environments.

items.headline: Adobe Launches Acrobat Productivity Agent for PDFs
items.summary: Adobe's new Acrobat agent, launched May 6, lets users chat with PDFs, extract insights, and generate decks, podcasts, and social posts directly from documents. For real estate operators drowning in OMs, leases, and inspection reports, agentic PDF tooling is the highest-ROI early use case.

items.headline: Xactly Launches Fleet of AI Agents at Upside 2026
items.summary: Revenue platform Xactly unveiled a Fleet of Agents and Intelligence Studio at its May 14 customer conference, targeting planning, compensation, and revenue ops. The pattern — vertical SaaS shipping agent fleets natively — is becoming the dominant 2026 enterprise AI roadmap.

items.headline: 42% of Tech Leaders Report Low or No ROI on AI Investments
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 Global Technology Leadership Study finds 42% of tech leaders report low or no ROI on AI spend so far, exposing how hard it is to translate model capability into business value. The mandate is shifting from 'operators' to 'orchestrators' — measuring AI by P&L impact rather than tools deployed.

items.headline: Hierarchies Are Flattening — Leadership Goes Lateral by 2030
items.summary: Economic pressure, AI-driven efficiencies, and a demand for agility are compressing org charts, with most organizations expected to have fewer management layers by 2030 as leadership disperses laterally across teams. The implication for operators: invest in player-coaches and cross-functional fluency, not deeper VP benches.

items.headline: 89% of Employees Perform Better When Prioritizing Wellbeing
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 Work-Life-Wellness report finds 89% of employees say their performance improves when they prioritize wellbeing — turning wellness from a perk into a measurable productivity lever. For senior living operators, the data reinforces that staff-facing wellness investments correlate to resident outcomes.

items.headline: 'Emotional Fitness' Emerges as a New Health Category for 2026
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit's 2026 trends report names emotional fitness — training resilience and cognitive performance like a muscle — as a defining new wellness category. Expect to see emotional fitness programming move into corporate benefits and senior living amenities over the next 18 months.

items.headline: Wellness Real Estate Is the Fastest-Growing Wellness Economy Segment
items.summary: Wellness real estate has become the fastest-growing sector in the broader wellness economy as AI-driven diagnostics and longevity-focused design move into housing. The convergence between health tech and residential design is a long-tail tailwind for senior living and active-adult operators that lean into wellness as an amenity, not a marketing line.
